channel.title: +Priya on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Priya on Vishvas's notes

item.title: ‘Nysa in India’ & the
item.description: Source: here. ‘Nysa in India’ & the Related Narratives Motivation Alexander III of Macedon in his campaign of world conquest reached India in 327 BCE after attaining victory over the Persian Achaemenid Empire. It is reported by western classical sources that in India, the invaders stumbled on a city named Nysa and the inhabitants of the city told him that it was founded by none other than Dionysus. Alexander, we are told, was elated; he had found descendants of Greeks, in a far away land, conquered by the divine.

item.title: A Stolen King: Alexander III of Macedon
item.description: Source: here. It’s June 323 BCE in Babylonia. In the grand palace of one of the most powerful Neo-Babylonian emperor of the past, Nebuchadnezzar II lies the lifeless body of another powerful monarch, Alexander III of Macedon. But the scorching heat of the Mesopotamian summer apparently does not take its toll on the body of the king. You see, in Alexander, the lines of history and myth tends to blur to the point of looking the same.
